metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC4 to require that each of the five tests both exists AND makes at least one behavioral assertion (e.g. 'asserts snapshot retained', 'asserts process survives parent exit'), explicitly ruling out skipped/stub tests.
findings.finding: AC4 bundles five independently-verifiable behavioral test scenarios into one criterion and asserts only existence ('tests exist'), not that each test exercises and asserts the specific contract named in the Success Criteria — leaving it unclear whether a skipped or assertion-free stub satisfies the AC.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 and Success Criteria — third bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'New tests exist for:' with 'New, non-skipped tests each assert the named contract:' and add one-line outcome descriptions per scenario mirroring the Success Criteria language (e.g. 'compactor process death — asserts rebar state is consistent after SIGKILL').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split AC item 4 into five separate, individually verifiable acceptance criteria — one per test type (rename fault injection, process death, async-push parent-exit, real remote-append interleaving, parent-cascade cross-clone) — each with its own grep-able name pattern.
findings.finding: AC item 4 bundles five distinct test deliverables into a single verifiable unit (grep for test names), making it impossible to determine which specific tests are present or absent if only a subset is implemented. A single AC item cannot be half-closed; partial delivery would appear as a full pass on the grep check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — item 4: 'New tests exist for: compaction rename fault injection, compactor process death, async-push parent-exit survival, real remote-append interleaving, and parent-cascade cross-clone races'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the single bundled bullet with five bullets, e.g.:
- [ ] A test matching `*rename_fault*` or `*fault_inject*compact*` exists and passes.
- [ ] A test matching `*compactor*process_death*` or `*compact*kill*` exists and passes.
- [ ] A test matching `*async_push*parent_exit*` exists and passes.
- [ ] A test matching `*remote_append*interleav*` exists and passes.
- [ ] A test matching `*parent_cascade*cross_clone*` exists and passes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-rollback schema-state assertion to the AC: specify that after a failed `os.remove(final_path)` the ticket directory must have the SNAPSHOT present AND all source events active (no retired files), and verify this invariant in the fault-injection test.
findings.finding: The plan does not assert the on-disk schema state after a partial rollback, only that a test 'asserting snapshot retention on incomplete rollback exists and passes'. When the reverse-rename succeeds but the subsequent `os.remove(final_path)` fails (OSError caught at line 245), the SNAPSHOT survives on disk alongside a mix of still-active source events and already-reversed renames — a valid recoverable state. But the plan never declares this state contract, so implementors have no specification to verify against and the AC test may only cover the happy-path snapshot-retained case without asserting what happens to the source events.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'src/rebar/_commands/compact.py no longer unconditionally removes the snapshot in its rollback branch; a fault-injection test asserting snapshot retention on incomplete rollback exists and passes.'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC to read: '…a fault-injection test asserting (a) snapshot retention on incomplete rollback AND (b) that all source-event files remain active (not retired) when the reverse-rename path is taken, AND (c) that all source-event files remain active when os.remove(final_path) raises OSError, exists and passes.'

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a coordination note to child 25aa (or 1d1b) specifying that whichever lands second must update the scope qualifier in docs/mutation-testing.md to reflect the expanded shard set.
findings.finding: child 1d1b and child 25aa both modify `docs/mutation-testing.md`, but with potentially contradictory framing: 1d1b qualifies existing scores as 'within the selected scope (5 cores)', while 25aa adds new shards beyond those 5 cores and records new scores in the same file. After 25aa lands, 1d1b's '5 cores' language will be stale, yet neither child is instructed to update or reconcile the other's wording.
findings.location: child 1d1b-d85f-dc34-41dd, AC bullet 3; child 25aa-1df0-5901-417b, AC bullet 3
findings.suggested_fix: In child 25aa's AC, add: 'Update the scope qualifier in docs/mutation-testing.md (introduced by 1d1b) to reflect the expanded shard set (no longer limited to the original 5 cores).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and test the post-failure state contract for `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` partial rename: either add a compensating-forward path (a subsequent fsck/rebuild idempotently completes retirement of residual active events) or add reverse logic mirroring `_compact_locked`, and add a fault-injection AC for this scenario explicitly.
findings.finding: The `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` function in compact.py has no rollback/reverse path if renames fail mid-loop (lines 389-396): the new SNAPSHOT is already atomically written before the rename loop begins, and rename failures are only warned-and-continued. If multiple renames fail, the ticket directory ends with the new SNAPSHOT + some still-active originals + partially-retired others, and no recovery is described. The plan schedules a 'compactor process death' test but does not assert the post-failure schema state for this function's partial-rename scenario.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'New tests exist for: compaction rename fault injection, compactor process death…'; rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log in src/rebar/_commands/compact.py lines 384-396

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a coordination note to one of 1d1b or f476 specifying which lands first and that the second author must rebase on the first's changes to docs/concurrency.md.
findings.finding: child 1d1b and child f476 both modify `docs/concurrency.md` — 1d1b adds a reference to the detached-HEAD regression test, while f476 adds a new ownership policy section — but no coordination between them is specified. If they land independently, merge conflicts or structural inconsistency in the document are likely.
findings.location: child 1d1b-d85f-dc34-41dd, Scope and AC bullet 1; child f476-1f56-48c9-487c, Scope and AC bullet 1
findings.suggested_fix: Since 1d1b is a pure docs-correction story and f476 adds a new section, prefer landing 1d1b first and noting in f476 that the author should rebase on 1d1b before editing concurrency.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either assign sync-branch and lock stale-owner/reclamation mutation shards to child 25aa (if behavioral tests are added there) or create a new sibling story that explicitly owns them and add it to the epic.
findings.finding: child 25aa explicitly descopes sync branch selection and lock stale-owner/reclamation mutation shards to 'a follow-up story,' but no such follow-up story exists in the sibling roster — those areas have no assigned owner within the epic. This is a partial-without-remainder pattern: 25aa covers a subset of the mutation-testing expansion and names an unscheduled remainder.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC to child 25aa: if behavioral tests for sync/lock are NOT added by 25aa, create a tracked follow-up ticket (referenced by ID in 25aa's description) before this story is closed, so the remainder has a concrete owner.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC4, state the required test-function name (or name pattern) for each of the five scenarios so the grep verification is deterministic.
findings.finding: AC4's verification clause 'grep-able test names present in the suite' defers the actual test-function naming decision to the executor — no names or name patterns are specified, so two different implementers could produce incompatible naming schemes and both satisfy the criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('New tests exist for…')
findings.suggested_fix: Append a parenthetical to each scenario naming the expected function, e.g. '`test_compact_rename_fault_forward`, `test_compact_rename_fault_reverse`', '`test_compactor_process_death`', '`test_async_push_parent_exit`', '`test_remote_append_real_interleave`', '`test_parent_cascade_cross_clone`' — or state a shared prefix such as `test_fault_` that all five must share.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / expand-contract note for the whitespace-title rejection: either a deprecation grace period, a feature flag, or a stated rollback procedure so consumers on all three surfaces (CLI, library, MCP) can recover if the stricter validation causes integration issues.
findings.finding: The whitespace-title rejection changes a currently-accepted input contract across three consumer surfaces (CLI, library, MCP) and is framed only as a policy decision — no expand-contract sequencing or back-out plan is stated, leaving consumers with no rollback path if the stricter validation causes integration breakage.
findings.location: Policy decisions section + AC item 2 ('rebar create/edit and the library/MCP create paths reject a whitespace-only title')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line 'Rollback: revert composer.py validation to `if not title` and redeploy' note in the plan, or stage the change as: (1) emit a warning for whitespace titles for one release cycle, then (2) hard-reject — whichever fits the project's release cadence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation target for the changed compaction rollback contract (snapshot retained on incomplete rollback) — either extend `docs/concurrency.md` or add a brief ADR/inline comment in `compact.py` — and list it in AC item 3.
findings.finding: The plan changes the compaction rollback contract in compact.py (snapshot is now retained rather than deleted on incomplete rollback), which is a new behavioral guarantee, but no documentation update is scheduled to record this changed contract — the doc-update AC covers only concurrency.md, README.md, and mutation-testing.md.
findings.location: AC item 1 ('src/rebar/_commands/compact.py no longer unconditionally removes the snapshot in its rollback branch') + AC item 3 (doc update scope)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC item 3: 'docs/concurrency.md (or compact.py module docstring) describes the new fault-atomic rollback guarantee: snapshot is retained on incomplete compaction rollback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a single shared utility or validation layer (e.g., a `validate_title()` helper in the library layer) that all three entry points (CLI, library, MCP) delegate to, so the whitespace-rejection rule has one authoritative home.
findings.finding: The whitespace-only title rejection rule must be enforced 'across CLI, library, and MCP' but the plan does not specify a single authoritative location (shared validator, base class, or utility function) for this rule. Without an explicit interface boundary, each entry point may implement the check independently, creating divergent logic and a future maintenance burden.
findings.location: AC line: 'rebar create/edit and the library/MCP create paths reject a whitespace-only title … across CLI, library, and MCP'; Policy decision section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC or child story: 'Whitespace validation is extracted into a single `_validate_title(title: str) -> None` function in the library core (e.g., `src/rebar/_core/validation.py`); CLI, library, and MCP entry points call it rather than each checking inline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan a positive rationale explaining why retaining the SNAPSHOT on rollback is correct: specifically, that when reverse-renames partially fail, the retained SNAPSHOT is the only covering record of the already-retired sources, the pre-lock SNAPSHOT-present guard makes subsequent compact calls a no-op, and the .retired idempotency pattern means the retained snapshot is safe.
findings.finding: The plan gives no positive rationale for why retaining the SNAPSHOT (rather than removing it) is the correct behavior when a rollback's reverse-renames also partially fail. The mechanism depends on a subtle system property — that the pre-lock SNAPSHOT-present guard prevents re-compaction and that partially-restored source files are benign alongside the SNAPSHOT — but this reasoning is absent from the plan, leaving implementers unable to verify the invariant holds.
findings.location: AC line 1 / Success Criteria ('The compaction fold is provably all-or-none')
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's approach description for the compact.py fix, add: 'Retaining the SNAPSHOT is correct because: (1) it is the only covering record of any already-retired sources when reverse-renames partially fail; (2) the pre-lock SNAPSHOT-present guard (compact_cli lines 497-500) prevents a subsequent compact from double-folding; (3) .retired idempotency (line 222-223) makes the partially-reversed sources benign. Remove the snapshot only when ALL reverse-renames succeed (full rollback to the pre-SNAPSHOT state).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the whitespace-only title fix and its test to cover `edit_core` (composer.py line ~380, `if value == '':` → `if not value.strip():`) and add an AC test asserting `rebar edit --title='   '` returns an error.
findings.finding: The AC for whitespace-only title rejection explicitly covers 'CLI, library, and MCP' create paths, but the edit path (edit_core in composer.py) has a separate title guard — `if value == ''` (line 380) — that also passes whitespace-only titles through. The Success Criteria says 'rebar create/edit ... reject a whitespace-only title' but the AC test assertion only names create paths, leaving the edit path's validation gap un-exercised and potentially unfixed.
findings.suggested_fix: In composer.py edit_core, change line 380 from `if value == '':` to `if not value.strip():` and add a test: `edit_core(ticket_id, {'title': '   '}, repo_root=...) raises CommandError`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'depends_on: 4001' (or equivalent ordering note) to child 44b7 so it is scheduled and implemented after the compact.py rollback restructuring is complete.
findings.finding: child 44b7 inserts a test-only failpoint hook inside `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py`'s rename loop, and child 4001 restructures the same rollback/rename code — yet no dependency between 44b7 and 4001 is stated. If either lands first the failpoint insertion point in 44b7 may be incompatible with 4001's restructured loop.
findings.location: child 44b7-2f9e-26ee-4835, Scope and Acceptance Criteria; child 4001-185c-1a51-44ee, Scope
findings.suggested_fix: In child 44b7's description or metadata, add: 'This story depends on 4001 landing first; the failpoint must be placed within the post-4001 rename loop structure.'

findings.finding: Child 25aa-1df0's rationale states mutation shards are needed 'once behavioral tests for those paths exist (S1, S3, S4),' specifically targeting 'sync branch selection, compaction fold/rollback decisions, push policy/retry classification, lock stale-owner/reclamation.' Of these four targets, only compaction fold/rollback (4001) and push retry (a263 touches push, but only the parent-exit path) and remote-append (1fdc) have sibling behavioral tests. Sync branch selection and lock stale-owner/reclamation have NO sibling that adds behavioral tests for them — 25aa's mutation shards would cover decision logic with no new behavioral test backing, leaving the 'shards target near-pure helpers covered by behavioral tests' rationale partially unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: In 25aa's scope, scope down to compaction fold/rollback and push retry classification (covered by sibling behavioral tests), and add a note that sync and lock shards are deferred until dedicated behavioral stories exist.

findings.finding: AC bullet 1 is an epic-of-epics pattern: 'created, plan-reviewed, AND closed' spans multiple separate sessions/reviews by definition and is not a single in-session completable outcome. Additionally, 'plan-reviewed' is a process milestone (a human approval step), not a repo-observable artifact, making it impossible to verify deterministically via CI or a command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, bullet 1: 'All child stories are created, plan-reviewed, and closed.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with: 'A tracking checklist in this issue (or a linked milestone) lists every child story; each child story's PR is merged and its issue is closed — verifiable via the milestone's closed-issue count reaching N.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
